arts.ttl: Ancelotti: “Real Madrid played very, very well for 60 minutes”
arts.sum: Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti spoke to the media after his team emerged 3-2 victors against Deportivo Alavés in La Liga on Tuesday night. Ancelotti on yellow cards for dissent

arts.ttl: Managing Madrid Podcast: Real Madrid 3 - 2 Alaves, Post-game, La Liga 2024 - 2025
arts.sum: On this episode of the Managing Madrid Podcast, Kiyan Sobhani is joined by Sam Leveridge on his walk home from the Bernabeu to discuss: Kiyan is joined by Sam to discuss a rollercoaster of a game at the Bernabeu

arts.ttl: Real Madrid starlet Endrick escaped a red card for a shocking challenge vs Alavés
arts.sum: Real Madrid striker Endrick was extremely lucky to escape a red card in the club’s latest outing on Tuesday. As much came owing to the actions of the Brazilian late…

arts.ttl: Real Madrid man 'already been told' he will win the 2024 Ballon d'Or
arts.sum: Real Madrid forward Vinicius Junior will take home the 2024 Ballon d'Or during its prize ceremony in Paris next month, according to a report.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid boss Ancelotti provides Kylian Mbappé injury update
arts.sum: Real Madrid head coach Carlo Ancelotti has tonight provided an update on the wellbeing of star attacker Kylian Mbappé. This comes, for those who may have missed it, after Mbappé…

arts.ttl: “It’s a clear red card” – Alaves manager Luis Garcia Plaza furious with decision during Real Madrid defeat
arts.sum: Real Madrid edged their way to a fifth successive victory on Tuesday, as they saw off a brave fightback from Alaves at the Santiago Bernabeu. The visitors scored twice late on to make things interesting, and it could have been even more nervy had Endrick Felipe been shown a red card just before the goals. […] The post “It’s a clear red card” – Alaves manager Luis Garcia Plaza furious with decision during Real Madrid defeat appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid 3-2 Alaves: Highlights, man of the match, stats
arts.sum: Real Madrid avoided an embarrassing late collapse to narrowly beat Alaves 3-2 as they conceded twice in a minute to make for a nervy end at the Estadio Santiago Bernabeu

arts.ttl: Mbappé extends scoring streak as Real Madrid beats Alaves 3-2 in Spanish league
arts.sum: Lucas Vázquez and Rodrygo also scored for Madrid, which extended its unbeaten run in the league to 39 matches — with 29 wins and 10 draws going back to last season. Mbappé's seventh goal since joining Madrid was a beauty, with the France forward starting a give-and-go with Jude Bellingham with a heal flick and then picking up the pass inside the area and using a nifty move to clear a defender before finding the net in the 40th minute at the Santiago Bernabeu Stadium.

arts.ttl: Three questions and three answers from Real Madrid 3-2 Alavés
arts.sum: Los Blancos took only 57 seconds to take the lead as Lucas Vázquez tapped in from close range, but Kylian Mbappé followed up to double the team’s lead with an impressive solo goal before half-time. 57 seconds was all it took for Lucas Vázquez to open the scoring for Real Madrid, scoring the first first-half goal that the team have scored since matchday one against Mallorca.

arts.ttl: Immediate Reaction: Real Madrid 3-2 Deportivo Alavés
arts.sum: Real Madrid 3-2 Deportivo Alavés (Vàzquez, Mbappé & Rodrygo). The fixture congestion meant that Real Madrid were back in action already, at home again to local rivals Deportivo Alavés.
